D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Metallic & Pearlescent Flakes

a metallic ink is a sea of tiny tilted mirrors — at any angle only the few aligned with your eye flash, and the others stay dark

Suspend tens of thousands of micro-flakes in a clear binder and you have metallic ink. Each flake reflects like a tiny mirror; only flakes whose normal happens to point near you flash bright. Aluminium flakes give a neutral silver sparkle; mica flakes are coated thin-film stacks that throw a hue at the lit angle and a different one elsewhere — pearlescent shimmer.

Drag the viewing angle and watch the sparkle pattern reshuffle — different flakes catch the light. Switch from aluminium to mica and the bright flakes carry a hue that travels across the spectrum as the angle changes.
